# Thevenins Theorem
# Northons Theorem

## Ex. 1
$E=20,J=0.5A,R=10Ω,R_{L}=20Ω$

$E_{0}^{I}=E·\frac{3R}{6R}=\frac{E}{2}=10V$
$I_{1}=J \frac{2R}{2R+R+3R}=\frac{J}{3}=\frac{0.5}{3}A=\frac{1}{6}A$
$E_{0}^{II}=-I_{1}·3R=-\frac{1}{6}A·3·10Ω=-5V$
$R_{0}=?=(R+2R)\parallel 3R=1.5R=15Ω$

$J_{0}=\underbrace{ J_{0}^{I} }_{ E }+\underbrace{ J_{0}^{II} }_{ J }=\frac{2}{3}A+\left( -\frac{1}{3}A \right)=\frac{1}{3}A$

$J_{0}^{I}=\frac{E}{3R}=\frac{20V}{30Ω}=\frac{2}{3}A$

$-J_{0}^{II}=J·\frac{2R}{R+2R}=\frac{2}{3}J$
$J_{0}^{II}=-\frac{2}{3}J=-\frac{2}{3}·0.5A=-\frac{1}{3}A$

$U=J_{0}·R_{0}=5V=E_{0}$
$I_{S}=\frac{E_{0}}{R_{0}}=J_{0}$
Open circuit voltage and internal restance

---

## Ex.2
Unknown linear source produces 16V when supplying current of 4 Amps, when current increases to 14 Amps output voltage drops to 3 Volts, calculate thevenin and northon models of this source
$16V,4A$
$3V,14A$
TH=? N=?
### Thevenin
$$
\begin{align}
 & \begin{cases}
16V=-R_{0}·4A+E_{0} \\
3V=-R_{0}·14A+E_{0}
\end{cases} \\
 & 16V=R_{0}·4A=3V+R_{0}·14A \\
 & 13V=R_{0}·10A \\
 & R_{0}=\frac{13V}{10A}=\underline{1.3Ω} \\
 &  \\
 & R_{0}=\frac{\Delta U}{\Delta I}=\frac{13V}{10A} \\
 &  \\
 & E_{0}=16V+1.3Ω·4A=\underline{21.2V} \\
 &  \\
 & I_{S}=\frac{E_{0}}{R_{0}}=J_{0}=\frac{21.2V}{1.3Ω}\underline{16.3A}
\end{align}
$$

This only work on paper since we can't have no resitance in reality
## Case 2
$R_{L}=0Ω \to P_{L}=I^{2}R_{L}=0W$
$R_{L} \to \infty \to P_{L}=\frac{U^{2}}{R_{L}}=0W$
$R_{L}=I^{2}R_{2}=\left( \frac{E_{0}}{R_{0}+R_{2}} \right)^{2}·R_{2}=E_{0}^{2} \frac{R_{L}}{(R_{0}+R_{2})^{2}}=P_{2}(R_{2})$

$\frac{E_{0}}{2R_{0}}=\frac{1}{2}·\overbrace{ \frac{E_{0}}{R_{0}} }^{ J_{0} }=\frac{J_{0}}{2}$
$P_{LMAX}=\frac{E_{0}J_{0}}{4}$
$P_{LMAX}=\frac{\left( \frac{E_{0}}{2} \right)^{2}}{R_{L}}=\frac{E_{0}^{2}}{4·R_{0}}=\left( \frac{J_{0}}{2} \right)^{2}·R_{L}=\frac{J_{0}^{2}}{4}·R_{0}$

TH.
$E_{0}=5V$
$R_{0}=10Ω$
This circuit is loaded by 2 resistors connected in pallarrer
$P_{2}=\underbrace{ R_{1} }_{ 25e } \parallel \underbrace{ \overbrace{ R_{2} }^{ ↑→ } }_{ ? }$

### EX.2.A
$R_{2}=? \to P_{R_{2}}\to MAX$
